text.title: AI Overview Extraction Rules
text.summary: Core structural rules for making content extractable by Google AI Overviews: answer placement, heading hierarchy, entity clarity, and semantic structure.

rule.statement: Place the direct answer to the heading's implied question within the first 1-2 sentences below that heading.
rule.rationale: Google AI extraction pattern reads the heading as a question and the following sentences as the answer. Burying the answer in paragraph 3 causes the AI to skip the page.

rule.statement: Keep direct answers to 1-2 sentences maximum; expand with context after the answer.
rule.rationale: AI Overviews surface the shortest extractable answer first. Dense paragraphs are not split by the extraction algorithm.

example.code:
What is schema markup?

Schema markup is structured data added to HTML that helps search engines understand the content type and relationships on a page. It uses vocabulary from schema.org.

Adding schema markup enables rich results and improves AI Overview citation probability...
example.note: Answer appears immediately after heading, in the first sentence.

example.code:
What is schema markup?

When building websites, there are many considerations for SEO. One important area that many developers overlook is structured data. In recent years, this has become increasingly important. Schema markup is structured data added to HTML...
example.note: Answer buried in paragraph 4 — AI extraction skips this.

rule.statement: Use H1 for the main topic, H2 for major sections (max 5-7), H3 for subsections; never skip heading levels.
rule.rationale: AI extraction maps heading levels to topic and subtopic relationships. Skipped levels break the structural inference.

rule.statement: Make each heading descriptive enough to stand alone as a question or topic statement.
rule.rationale: Headings are used as query-matching anchors; vague headings ("Overview", "Details") do not match user queries.

rule.statement: Define the main entity in the first paragraph of the page; answer "what is X?" immediately.
rule.rationale: If AI cannot map the content to a specific entity or fact in the knowledge graph, it will not cite the page.

rule.statement: Use consistent entity naming throughout the page; define jargon and acronyms on first use.
rule.rationale: Entity ambiguity causes the AI to assign low confidence and skip the source.

rule.statement: Cite all statistics with source name and publication date within the same sentence.
rule.rationale: AI Overviews prefer verifiable claims with attributable sources; uncited statistics are discounted.

rule.statement: Use short paragraphs (3-4 sentences max), bullet lists for enumerated items, and tables for comparisons.
rule.rationale: AI extraction breaks on dense paragraphs with complex embedded sentences. Lists and tables map cleanly to AI output structures.

rule.statement: Write in plain language; avoid passive voice and ambiguous phrasing.
rule.rationale: Entity clarity drops when subjects are obscured by passive constructions.
